content.full_text:
r---PC

JUL 3 1 1992
RECEIVED

14865 Panoche Road
Paicines, CA 95043
July 28, 1992

The Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Sirs:

The Willow Grove Union School District is a small rural school district located in
Paicines, California. Since this area is sparsely populated and our non-certificated
staff is hired locally, it is difficult to find board members that are free of any potential
conflict of interest.

The San Benito County Grand Jury requested that several rural schools explore the
possibility that they are in violation of the rules governing conflicts of interest. Willow
Grove was one of those districts, as we have a board member whose wife is employed
by the district. County Counsel rendered the opinion that since the initial employment
by the district began two years prior to the husband's election to the board of trustees a
conflict of interest does not exist in this case. However, County Counsel was
concerned about a potential conflict of interest involving our budgetary process. We
ask for an opinion from your commission regarding the propriety of the husband voting
on a budget that includes his wife's wages. Also we would ask for your suggestions as
to actions necessary to eliminate any conflict of interest.

Thank you for your help with this matter.

Sincerely yours,

Martin B. Miller, President
Willow Grove Union School Board

MBM/ba

sections.question: We ask for an opinion from your commission regarding the propriety of the husband voting on a budget that includes his wife's wages. Also we would ask for your suggestions as to actions necessary to eliminate any conflict of interest.
sections.question_synthetic: May a school board member vote on a budget that includes compensation for his wife who is employed by the district, and what actions are necessary to eliminate any potential conflict of interest?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or advice section present. The letter clearly states the factual situation and poses specific questions seeking FPPC opinion. Document has informal structure without typical Q&A headers.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: We ask for an opinion from your commission regarding the propriety of the husband voting on a budget that includes his wife's wages. Also we would ask for your suggestions as to actions necessary to eliminate any conflict of interest.

Since this area is sparsely populated and our non-certificated staff is hired locally, it is difficult to find board members that are free of any potential conflict of interest. The San Benito County Grand Jury requested that several rural schools explore the possibility that they are in violation of the rules governing conflicts of interest. Willow Grove was one of those districts, as we have a board member whose wife is employed by the district. County Counsel rendered the opinion that since the initial employment by the district began two years prior to the husband's election to the board of trustees a conflict of interest does not exist in this case. Miller, President Willow Grove Union School Board MBM/ba
embedding.summary: Willow Grove Union School District requests FPPC guidance on whether a board member may vote on budgets containing his wife's wages as a district employee, following a Grand Jury inquiry into potential conflicts of interest.
